commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: Protected maintainer labeling, missing native Windows runtime proof, and the older same-fix PR make this a maintainer validation and canonical-PR choice rather than an automated repair job.; Cleared: The diff narrows a shell-exec path on Windows and adds test/changelog coverage without adding dependencies, CI changes, secrets handling, or new execution surfaces. (sha=238a088bbe7780e2104efa1f532f7d7b9024849d)
commands.review_summary:
The PR makes the shell-env fallback return an empty successful probe on `win32`, threads a platform override through the helper, adds focused regression coverage, and updates the changelog.

Reproducibility: yes. for a high-confidence source path: current main config loading can call `loadShellEnvFallback`, and the probe resolves and executes the login-shell path without a win32 guard when expected keys are missing. I did not run a native Windows failing smoke in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Protected maintainer labeling, missing native Windows runtime proof, and the older same-fix PR make this a maintainer validation and canonical-PR choice rather than an automated repair job.

target.title: fix(config): skip shell env fallback on Windows
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #84795 by making the optional login-shell env fallback a no-op on native Windows before it can resolve or execute `/bin/sh`.

The existing POSIX behavior is unchanged. `getShellPathFromLoginShell` already skipped `win32`; this applies the same platform boundary to the direct `loadShellEnvFallback` path used during config loading.

## Verification

2026-05-23 update after rebasing onto `origin/main` (`acf265d4d5`), head `8ef82383f5`:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/shell-env.test.ts --reporter=verbose` (1 file, 21 tests passed)
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md src/infra/shell-env.ts src/infra/shell-env.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/infra/shell-env.ts src/infra/shell-env.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`

Earlier verification before the rebase:

- `corepack pnpm install`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md src/infra/shell-env.ts src/infra/shell-env.test.ts`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/infra/shell-env.ts src/infra/shell-env.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- Testbox-through-Crabbox, provider `blacksmith-testbox`, id `tbx_01ksam41ew6rr7b0zmwf5hacq7`, Actions run https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26335373158:
  `CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 OPENCLAW_TESTBOX_REMOTE_RUN=1 corepack pnpm test src/infra/shell-env.test.ts`
  - Result: 1 file / 21 tests passed.
- Autoreview: `/mnt/c/src/claws-hapi/.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`
  - Result: clean, no accepted/actionable findings.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: With `OPENCLAW_LOAD_SHELL_ENV=1` or config-driven shell env import enabled on native Windows, OpenClaw should not try to execute `/bin/sh` and should not emit `[openclaw] shell env fallback failed: spawnSync /bin/sh ENOENT`.

Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu-24.04 checkout at `/root/src/openclaw-85739`, Node/Vitest via repo wrapper. Earlier proof also ran on Blacksmith Testbox through Crabbox (`provider=blacksmith-testbox`, id `tbx_01ksam41ew6rr7b0zmwf5hacq7`, GitHub Actions run https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26335373158).

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/shell-env.test.ts --reporter=verbose`

Evidence after fix: The `shell-env` regression test calls `loadShellEnvFallback` with `platform: "win32"`, a missing expected key, and an `exec` stub that would throw `spawnSync /bin/sh ENOENT` if the POSIX shell probe ran. The result is `{ ok: true, applied: [] }`, the exec stub is not called, and no warning is logged.

Observed result after fix: The native Windows platform branch returns an empty shell-env probe result before shell resolution, so no `/bin/sh` process is attempted and no fallback warning is emitted. The focused suite passed 21 tests.

What was not tested: I did not complete a full native Windows `openclaw status` smoke from this WSL worktree because Windows Node was blocked from importing files from the WSL filesystem with `Access is denied`. The tested behavior is the exact internal fallback branch that caused the warning.
target.author: giodl73-repo

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
